div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in Liberia (Walton)
div.p:
Sir: The Department regards with satisfaction the signature of a treaty of commerce and navigation between the United States and
Liberia on August 8, 1938,Department of State Treaty Series No. 956, or 54 Stat. 1739. a consular convention on October 7, 1938,Department of State Treaty Series No. 957, or 54 Stat. 1751. and an extradition treaty on November 1, 1937.Department of State Treaty Series No. 955, or 54 Stat. 1733. These three instruments were transmitted to the Senate by the President on February 13, 1939, with a view to receiving the advice and consent of the Senate to ratification.

With a view towards completing a series of treaties deemed suitable for the regulation of the relations between the two republics, there are enclosed herewith two copies of a draft conciliation conventionNot printed. which is practically identical with conciliation conventions now in force between the United States and nineteen other countries.

As you doubtless know, an arbitration treaty between the United States and Liberia signed on February 10, 1926Department of State Treaty Series No. 747, or 44 Stat. 2438. is now in force. This Government would be pleased to enter into a conciliation convention with Liberia as part of its program looking towards the peaceful settlement of international disputes.
You are authorized to present the enclosed draft convention to the Liberian authorities at such time as you may deem appropriate.
div.closer:
For the Secretary of State:
R. Walton Moore
